_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 22:55:52Z)**_

The change adds a `step === 1 &&` guard to a JSX block in a static React prototype (`docs/vercel_mockup/`) — matching the existing comment that the section is "visible to the officer in step 1". All rendered values (`b.ref`, `b.passenger.name`, `b.rejectionReason`, etc.) flow into React text children, which auto-escape; there is no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, no DOM sink, no fetch/exec/eval, no auth boundary, and the file is a mockup not deployed as an authoritative backend. No source→sink path is introduced or weakened.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 22:55:56Z)**_

No security findings. The diff is a one-line client-side render guard (`step === 1 && ...`) inside a Vercel mockup JSX file. It narrows when a list of officer-owned bookings is displayed, doesn't introduce any new sink (no HTML injection, no exec, no auth/permission boundary, no IPC), and all interpolated values are rendered as React text children (auto-escaped). No vulnerabilities.
